# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow that runs on self-hosted runners in our datacenter. The workflow executes custom build scripts and deployment commands provided by developers. This gives our team flexibility to run any build process they need on our infrastructure.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Custom Build Workflow with GitHub Actions 
on: [push, pull_request] # Triggered when a file changes in the repository (default workflow) or an event trigger is received such as `pull request` by someone else modifying it.
jobs:    
  <job-name> :  
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest  // Required to run on latest version of Ubuntu server with minimal setup, could be a custom VM for faster testing purposes also if you want more flexibility e.g Virtual Machine image is recommended as well in the case when running self managed runner which doesn't have all dependencies installed yet
    steps:  
     - uses: actions/checkout@v2 // Fetches latest version of repository state – required to reference external repos and pass inputs with workflow commands. Use 'repository_dispatch', see below for sending an update on the status via webhooks (this will only work if you have set up GitHub's web-hook in your repo)
      - name: Check out repository   // Print useful information using .env(${{ secrets.name }})  e.g., git checkout $GITHUB_REPOSITORY, the ref being passed via inputs to workflow or step is used here ie 'refs/heads/{branch-name}' (replace { branch name} with appropriate variable)
       # Add custom build scripts and deployment commands into this section. The steps below use Git Bash terminal on Windows machine but could be different if using other shells e,g., PowerShell in case of self managed runner or Docker container runners for example – add relevant lines to execute the script (commands) you want here
      - name: Build and Test   // Explain what this job does. You can replace with your actual build command if necessary(s). If using other languages like Go, NodeJS etc., it should be written inside `script` section e..g.:  "go test ./..." or another relevant commands in respective language
      - name: Deploy   // Similar to 'Build and Test' but for deployment specific steps (commands) here. It could include staging/production deployments, etc., add your command(s). Here is an example line of code e.: "npm run build" or another relevant commands in respective language
